entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The primary crop evaluated for grain mould resistance.

entities.name: GRAIN MOULD
entities.description: A fungal disease affecting the grains of sorghum.

entities.name: GRAIN MOULD RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum genotypes to resist grain mould infection.

entities.name: SEED HARDNESS
entities.description: A morphological trait of the seed that strongly contributes to grain mould resistance.

entities.name: SEED PHENOL CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of phenols in the seed, which strongly affects grain mould response.

entities.name: GLUME COLOUR
entities.description: The color of the glumes, which strongly affects grain mould response.

entities.name: SEED COLOUR
entities.description: The color of the seeds, which has a relatively less effect on grain mould response.

entities.name: SEED FLAVAN-4-OL CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of flavan-4-ols in the seed, which has a relatively less effect on grain mould response.

entities.name: GLUME PHENOL CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of phenols in the glumes, which has a relatively less effect on grain mould response.

entities.name: GLUME FLAVAN-4-OL CONTENT
entities.description: The concentration of flavan-4-ols in the glumes, which has a relatively less effect on grain mould response.

entities.name: GLUME COVER
entities.description: The extent of glume coverage, which has a relatively less effect on grain mould response.

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: The geographical region where hard-seeded guinea sorghum lines are sources of resistance.

entities.name: SOUTH ASIA
entities.description: The geographical region where white-seeded sorghum is targeted for improvement.

entities.name: GUINEA SORGHUM
entities.description: A specific type of sorghum known for hard seeds and grain mould resistance in West Africa.

entities.name: WHITE-SEEDED SORGHUM
entities.description: A specific type of sorghum targeted for grain mould resistance improvement in South Asia.
